title: Schizachyrium neoscoparium
taxonomy: Schizachyrium neoscoparium is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is native to the southeastern United States. This plant belongs to the genus Schizachyrium, which is a group of clump-forming grasses. The family Poaceae, to which Schizachyrium neoscoparium belongs, is one of the largest and most diverse families of flowering plants. The classification of Schizachyrium neoscoparium reflects its evolutionary relationships with other grasses.

intro: Schizachyrium neoscoparium, also known as Little Bluestem, is a perennial grass native to the southeastern United States. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the treatment of fever, sore throat, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Schizachyrium neoscoparium include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Little Bluestem is often prepared as a tea or infused into a salve, and is sometimes used to make a cough syrup or topical ointment.
